MongoDB develops a leading non-relational database platform used by enterprises building cloud and AI applications. Despite recent software sector weakness showcased by the iShares Expanded Tech-Software Sector ETF (IGV) down over 30% from its peak, MongoDB continues to post strong subscription growth and improving profitability metrics, supporting high Quant Growth and Revisions grades. But first, like the other stocks in this list, we’ll start with the Valuation metrics.

The PEG ratio tends to have stronger predictive value for performance than P/E, and MDB’s metrics demonstrate its Quant Valuation Grade. Although the 74.14 P/E is more than 3x the sector median, the forward PEG of 0.58 is nearly a 60% discount to the sector’s 1.40. The growth side of the story is told through MongoDB’s forward EBITDA growth of 24.21%, which sits more than 64% above the sector median, and its incredible long-term earnings growth (3-5Y CAGR) of 128.63%, which is nearly 8x the sector median.

As enterprises modernize data architectures and embed AI into workflows, MongoDB’s recurring revenue model offers durability and scale. The Fire Horse theme of speed and innovation aligns with its strong forward growth potential. From software intelligence, we conclude by returning to a small-cap Chinese growth stock with strong growth potential.

5. Waterdrop Inc. (WDH)

  • Market Capitalization: $654.61M

  • Quant Rating: Strong Buy

  • Quant Sector Ranking (as of 02/20/2026): 76 out of 673

  • Quant Industry Ranking (as of 02/20/2026): 1 out of 22

  • Sector: Financials

  • Industry: Insurance Brokers

Waterdrop Inc. operates a digital insurance marketplace in China, leveraging technology and data analytics to connect consumers with health and life insurance products. Following a period of regulatory tightening and macro uncertainty, Waterdrop has regained profitability momentum through cost discipline, improving margins, and solid policy growth. Its asset-light platform provides operating leverage as China’s middle class expands demand for private insurance solutions. Sticking with the same valuation-growth combination, WDH’s key metrics provide room for the price to run higher in 2026.

On a forward GAAP basis, WDH’s 10.02 P/E offers a 11.13% discount to the sector median. Meanwhile, the forward Price/Book ratio is 0.01 for WDH, representing nearly a 24% discount to peers. This is the gold standard valuation metric for an insurance company, as it shows value relative to the capital held to pay claims. On the growth side, WDH boasts an 'A+' Quant grade, supported by an outstanding forward EBITDA Growth measure of 306.86%, which is over 26x the sector median. Looking further ahead, WDH’s long-term earnings growth (3-5Y CAGR) of nearly 244% is more than 17x the sector.

In a Year of the Fire Horse, symbolizing bold recovery and accelerating pace, WDH reflects a rebound story fueled by digital adoption and rising growth prospects, leading naturally into our broader conclusion on selective momentum in 2026.

Conclusion: Harnessing the Fire Horse Momentum

The Year of the Fire Horse represents amplified energy, speed, and bold forward motion, an apt metaphor for 2026’s market potential. While technology and financials have borne the brunt of recent volatility, select companies with strong Quant Growth Factor grades and improving earnings trajectories stand ready to outperform, suggesting a dip-buying opportunity. From fintech platforms and education innovators in China to AI infrastructure, cloud software, and digital insurance marketplaces, FUTU, TAL, CRDO, MDB, and WDH embody the "double fire" dynamic of high power paired with forward growth opportunity. In what's shaping up to be a stock picker’s year, temporary market weakness may offer disciplined investors the chance to buy quality momentum names poised to gallop ahead as sentiment stabilizes.

We have many stocks with strong buy recommendations, and you can filter them using Stock Screens to suit your specific investment objectives. Alternatively, Alpha Picks might be ideal if you're interested in two monthly stock picks of the top "strong buy" Quant stocks. Seeking Alpha’s Quant ratings and investment research tools help to ensure you are furnished with the best resources to make informed investment decisions while taking the emotion out of investing.

If you’re looking for more frequent investment ideas, we launched the PRO Quant Portfolio in June. It’s a weekly rebalanced selection of our Top 30 Quant Strong Buy stocks, spanning multiple regions and market caps. Happy investing!
